{{nihongo|'''Kansha Shrine'''|官舎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kansha Shrine'''|官舎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kansha Shrine'''|官舎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kansha Shrine'''|官舎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

拝殿 | 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町本町1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 9月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=官舎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''官舎神社'''（かんしゃじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。元来は、「みやけじんじゃ」と呼ばれていたが、現在は、「かんしゃじんじゃ」又は、「離宮さん」{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|天平勝宝|Q1373169}}8年に、{{jalink|奈良|Q11444975}}の{{jalink|春日社}}を{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として{{jalink|勧請}}して祀ったのが始まり{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 官舎神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}。中世に廃絶した後、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}3年に再興する{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治13年、小俣神社（八柱神社）を合祀。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年、県社に昇格する{{Cite web|title=三重県 神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神：{{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 * 配祀神：{{jalink|多紀理毘売命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|多岐都比売命}}・{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天之菩卑能命}}・{{jalink|天津日子根命}}・{{jalink|活津日子根命}}・{{jalink|熊野久須毘命}}・{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|大田命}}・{{jalink|大宮女神}}・{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|金刀比羅神}}・不詳一座 === 例祭 === *9月15日 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '三重県伊勢市小俣町本町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:官舎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q135039271}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 官舎神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kansha Shrine.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町本町1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 9月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=官舎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''官舎神社'''（かんしゃじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。元来は、「みやけじんじゃ」と呼ばれていたが、現在は、「かんしゃじんじゃ」又は、「離宮さん」{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|天平勝宝|Q1373169}}8年に、{{jalink|奈良|Q11444975}}の{{jalink|春日社}}を{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として{{jalink|勧請}}して祀ったのが始まり{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 官舎神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}。中世に廃絶した後、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}3年に再興する{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治13年、小俣神社（八柱神社）を合祀。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年、県社に昇格する{{Cite web|title=三重県 神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神：{{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 * 配祀神：{{jalink|多紀理毘売命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|多岐都比売命}}・{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天之菩卑能命}}・{{jalink|天津日子根命}}・{{jalink|活津日子根命}}・{{jalink|熊野久須毘命}}・{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|大田命}}・{{jalink|大宮女神}}・{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|金刀比羅神}}・不詳一座 === 例祭 === *9月15日 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

拝殿 | 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町本町1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 9月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=官舎神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''官舎神社'''（かんしゃじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|伊勢市|Q328067}}小俣町に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。元来は、「みやけじんじゃ」と呼ばれていたが、現在は、「かんしゃじんじゃ」又は、「離宮さん」{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}。 === 由緒 === {{jalink|天平勝宝|Q1373169}}8年に、{{jalink|奈良|Q11444975}}の{{jalink|春日社}}を{{jalink|中臣氏|Q1198447}}の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}として{{jalink|勧請}}して祀ったのが始まり{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁 官舎神社|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}。中世に廃絶した後、{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}3年に再興する{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}12年、{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。明治13年、小俣神社（八柱神社）を合祀。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年、県社に昇格する{{Cite web|title=三重県 神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神：{{jalink|建御雷之男神}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}・{{jalink|天児屋根命}}・姫神 * 配祀神：{{jalink|多紀理毘売命}}・{{jalink|市杵島姫命}}・{{jalink|多岐都比売命}}・{{jalink|猿田彦神}}・{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|速須佐之男命}}・{{jalink|天忍穂耳命}}・{{jalink|天之菩卑能命}}・{{jalink|天津日子根命}}・{{jalink|活津日子根命}}・{{jalink|熊野久須毘命}}・{{jalink|倉稲魂命}}・{{jalink|大己貴命}}・{{jalink|大田命}}・{{jalink|大宮女神}}・{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}・{{jalink|金刀比羅神}}・不詳一座 === 例祭 === *9月15日 === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}} === 脚注 === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Haiden | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Ise|Q328067}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined|Q1793334}} in Obata-chō, {{jalink|Ise|Q328067}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{jalink|Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169}} 8, when {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine}} in {{jalink|Nara|Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}} of the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{jalink|Tagiribime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiya-no-Megami}}, {{jalink|Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Kotohira-no-Kami}}, and one unspecified seat === Annual festival === *September 15 === Related items === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Ise|Q328067}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined|Q1793334}} in Obata-chō, {{jalink|Ise|Q328067}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{jalink|Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169}} 8, when {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine}} in {{jalink|Nara|Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}} of the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{jalink|Tagiribime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiya-no-Megami}}, {{jalink|Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Kotohira-no-Kami}}, and one unspecified seat === Annual festival === *September 15 === Related items === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden | 所在地 = {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}{{jalink|Ise|Q328067}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{jalink|enshrined|Q1793334}} in Obata-chō, {{jalink|Ise|Q328067}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki shrine}}, and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{jalink|Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169}} 8, when {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine}} in {{jalink|Nara|Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{jalink|ancestral deity|Q85882782}} of the {{jalink|Nakatomi clan|Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{jalink|Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{jalink|Tagiribime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sarutahiko-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ikatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōta-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ōmiya-no-Megami}}, {{jalink|Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323}}, {{jalink|Kotohira-no-Kami}}, and one unspecified seat === Annual festival === *September 15 === Related items === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]]

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiribime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-no-Megami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotohira-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and one unspecified seat === Annual festival === *September 15 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}{{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q1793334|WD=Q1793334}} in Obata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiribime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikatsu-hikone-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano-Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōta-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōmiya-no-Megami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotohira-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and one unspecified seat === Annual festival === *September 15 === Related items === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}} === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんしやしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kansha Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q2564508}}, Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q1793334|WD=Q1793334|1=Boca do Acre}} in Obata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975|1=Nara}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami * Associated deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tagiribime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q1793334|WD=Q1793334|1=Boca do Acre}} in Obata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975|1=Nara}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q1793334|WD=Q1793334|1=Boca do Acre}} in Obata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975|1=Nara}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} Obata-chō Honmachi 1446 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-O-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}, Himegami | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = September 15 | 神事 = | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=Kansha Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kansha Shrine''' (かんしゃじんじゃ) is a shrine {{ill|lt=enshrined|Q1793334|WD=Q1793334|1=Boca do Acre}} in Obata-chō, {{ill|lt=Ise|Q328067|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975|1=Nara}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities: It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Originally it was called “Miyake Jinja,” but now it is called “Kansha Jinja” or “Rikyū-san”{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=https://genbu.net/data/ise/kansya_title.htm|access-date=2025-12-2}}. === History === It began in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-Shōhō|Q1373169|WD=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 8, when {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Nara|Q11444975|WD=Q11444975|1=Nara}} was invited and enshrined as the {{ill|lt=ancestral deity|Q85882782|WD=Q85882782|1=Oyagami}} of the {{ill|lt=Nakatomi clan|Q1198447|WD=Q1198447|1=Nakatomi clan}}{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines: Kansha Shrine|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-2}}. After falling into ruin in the medieval period, it was revived in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3{{Cite web|title=官舎神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is080151-01.html|access-date=2025-12-2}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12 it became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. In Meiji 13, Obata Shrine (Yahashira Shrine) was merged. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 19, it was promoted to prefectural shrine{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%ae%98%e8%88%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-27}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities:
